description_long: This bungalow features a traditional thatched roof made from dried palm or grass, extending well beyond the walls to provide ample shade and protection from rain. The walls are constructed from woven bamboo or similar natural materials, giving the structure a rustic and tropical appearance. Wooden beams frame the edges and support the roof, while the base of the walls is reinforced with a thicker wooden trim. One side of the bungalow includes a small, open porch area with a simple railing, and a single rectangular window is visible on one wall. The overall design is open and airy, suitable for warm climates, and the natural materials blend seamlessly with outdoor environments. The structure is single-story and compact, with a focus on simplicity and functionality.
description: A single-story bungalow with a thatched roof, woven bamboo walls, and a small open porch, designed in a rustic tropical style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide thatched roof overhanging the woven bamboo wall, with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable roof, wooden framing, and a small porch area with a partial railing.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the thatched roof, with the open porch and entryway partially visible beneath the eaves.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gable roof, a single rectangular window, and the woven bamboo wall construction.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with a single side window
